message:
perf(mvcc): gate notify_all_waiters / notify_waiters_for_page on empty waiter_count

Adds a single `waiter_count: AtomicUsize` to `InProcessPageLockTable` that
is incremented inside `register_waiter` (after the shard-mutex insert) and
decremented in `unregister_waiter`, `notify_waiters_for_page`, and the
slow body of `notify_all_waiters`. Both notification helpers now consult
the counter via a SeqCst-fenced `Relaxed` load and skip the per-shard
mutex acquisition when no thread is parked.

Synchronization is Dekker on paired SeqCst fences:
  * `register_waiter` sequences `fetch_add(Relaxed)` before a SeqCst fence
    before the caller's holder-recheck (in `wait_for_holder_change`).
  * `notify_*_waiters` sequences the caller's release CAS on
    `fast_locks` / shard slot before a SeqCst fence before the
    `waiter_count.load(Relaxed)` decision.
Either the notifier observes `waiter_count >= 1` and takes the slow path,
or the waiter observes the released holder and never parks.
Over-counting (e.g., from a panicking waiter that skipped
`unregister_waiter`) is safe — it only forces the slow path.

Microbench (`bench_notify_waiters_no_waiters`, n=7 trials):
  notify_all_waiters     1057.8 ns/call → 8.2 ns/call   (-99.2%, ~129x)
  notify_waiters_for_page  12.4 ns/call → 8.2 ns/call   (-33.9%)

The `notify_all_waiters` saving comes from skipping 256 per-shard
parking_lot mutex acquires + 256 empty `HashMap::drain` calls per
`release_all` / `release_set`. Closes the 0.29% MT8 self-time line for
`notify_all_waiters` flagged in docs/perf-mvcc-session-20260424.md and
also nicks the per-page release path that calls
`notify_waiters_for_page`.

Correctness: 1345 mvcc lib tests pass, 7 fail. Same 7 failures occur
on baseline (pre-existing concurrent-agent work in shared_lock_table /
lifecycle / history_compression / begin_concurrent — unrelated to
waiter shards). All four `wait_for_*` tests pass: wakes_on_release,
times_out for both holder-change and release-progress paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
